title: To Mary Campbell.
content:
I.

        Will ye go to the Indies, my Mary,
            And leave old Scotia's shore?
        Will ye go to the Indies, my Mary,
            Across th' Atlantic's roar?

II.

        O sweet grows the lime and the orange,
            And the apple on the pine;
        But a' the charms o' the Indies
            Can never equal thine.

III.

        I hae sworn by the Heavens to my Mary,
            I hae sworn by the Heavens to be true;
        And sae may the Heavens forget me
            When I forget my vow!

IV.

        O plight me your faith, my Mary,
            And plight me your lily white hand;
        O plight me your faith, my Mary,
            Before I leave Scotia's strand.

V.

        We hae plighted our troth, my Mary,
            In mutual affection to join;
        And curst be the cause that shall part us!
            The hour and the moment o' time!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Burns delivers a powerful performance in "To Mary Campbell."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
